response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has extensive experience in software development, including some C/C++ work and concurrent programming, but his focus has been primarily on web, mobile, and scripting languages (TypeScript, Node.js, Dlang, Java). His resume lacks clear evidence of deep C++ expertise, secure development lifecycle practices, or industrial automation/security standards alignment (e.g., IEC 62443). While he demonstrates strong architectural and performance optimization skills, his profile does not align closely enough with the core C++ and industrial cybersecurity requirements of the role.
strengths: Extensive experience in high-performance systems and concurrent programming (e.g., low-latency apps with Dlang, Asynchronous I/O)
Strong background in architectural design, scalability, and modular solutions (e.g., GISCollective, Direct Mail automation platform)
Proven ability to work across multiple languages and technologies, adapting to project needs (e.g., C/C++ for LabWindows, Node.js, TypeScript)
concerns: Limited recent and deep C++ experience (last significant C/C++ work was in 2015, with minimal evidence of STL or industrial-strength C++ development)
No clear evidence of secure development lifecycle (SDL) implementation or IEC 62443-4-1 compliance
Lack of experience in industrial automation, IoT interfaces (MQTT/REST), or industrial protocols (Modbus TCP/IP, IEC 61850, etc.)
